description_long: This is a small passerine bird with an olive-green back, yellowish underparts, and subtle white markings on the wings and face. The bird has a short, conical beak suited for seed eating, and its head features a faint white eyebrow stripe. The wings are compact and the tail is relatively long and slightly curved, tapering to a rounded tip. The legs are slender and appear to be a muted gray or brown color. The plumage is smooth with a soft, matte finish, and the overall body shape is streamlined for agile flight. This bird would be commonly seen perched in urban parks, gardens, or on street trees.
description: A small olive-green and yellow songbird with a conical beak, rounded tail, and subtle white facial markings. Its plumage is smooth and matte, with a streamlined body and slender legs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the olive-green back, yellowish belly, and the bird's streamlined body with a slightly curved tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the narrow body, the length and slight curve of the tail, and the muted coloration of the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same olive-green and yellow coloration, with the beak and facial markings visible.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the conical beak, white eyebrow stripe, and the symmetry of the head and body.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid olive-green upperparts with pale yellow underparts and faint white facial markings
